key_insight: High-quality (111)-textured SrTiO3 thin films can be grown on Pt(111)/Al2O3 substrates, with film quality strongly improved by using an elevated-temperature Pt template.
engineering_relevance: These low-roughness STO films are promising as epitaxial templates and dielectric layers for oxide electronics and capacitor structures.
method: RF magnetron sputter deposition of stoichiometric SrTiO3, 20–30 nm thick
Compared STO growth on Pt(111) templates prepared at room temperature versus elevated temperature
Characterized crystal orientation and surface morphology with XRD, rocking curve analysis, and AFM
evidence_signal: XRD confirmed (111)/(222) texture, the rocking-curve FWHM reached 0.2°, and AFM measured surface roughness below 1 nm.
watch_item: The reported film quality depends on the underlying Pt template quality, so process control of Pt growth temperature and texture is critical for reproducibility.
paper_title: Growth of (111)-textured SrTiO3 thin films on Pt(111)/Al2O3(1-102) substrates by rf magnetron sputter deposition
